items.text: Did your shortness of breath come on gradually over days, or suddenly over minutes to hours?
Do you need to prop yourself up on extra pillows to breathe comfortably at night?
choices: Needs one extra pillow to breathe comfortably

items.text: Have you ever woken up suddenly gasping for air that got better after you sat up?
choices: Wakes gasping for air, relieved by sitting up

items.text: Do you have any chest discomfort along with the shortness of breath, and if so, what does it feel like?
choices: Exertional pressure or tightness relieved by rest
Pleuritic pain, sharp and worse with inspiration
Sudden tearing pain radiating to the back

items.text: Have you had a cough?
Are you coughing anything up, and if so, what does it look like?
Have you noticed any wheezing?
choices: Wheeze consistent with prior diagnosed asthma or COPD
New wheeze with no prior reactive-airway history

items.text: Have you had a fever?
choices: Fever with shaking chills or rigors

items.text: Have you been told you have heart failure, and does this episode feel like a previous flare-up?
choices: Known heart failure, this episode feels different from baseline
Known heart failure, this episode resembles a prior decompensation

items.text: Have you broken out in hives, or noticed swelling of your lips, tongue, or throat after being exposed to something new?
choices: Hives after a new exposure, no airway symptoms
Lip, tongue, or throat swelling after a new exposure

items.text: Did you choke or aspirate on food or liquid right before this started?
Have you noticed any swelling in one leg?
Have you traveled recently, had surgery, been hospitalized, or do you have cancer or a history of blood clots?
choices: Air travel over four hours in the past month
Surgery or hospitalization in the past month
Active malignancy or prior venous thromboembolism

items.text: Does this feel like a panic attack, with tingling around your mouth or hands that gets better once you calm down?
choices: No situational trigger or panic history
Situational trigger with paresthesias, resolves with reassurance
Known panic disorder with similar prior episodes

items.text: Have you been told you have anemia, and has your breathlessness been gradually getting worse with activity?
choices: Known anemia with gradually worsening exertional dyspnea

items.text: Have you noticed black, tarry stools, red blood in your stool, or vomited blood?
Assess respiratory rate and work of breathing.
choices: Normal rate, no accessory muscle use
Severe distress with paradoxical or fatiguing respiratory pattern

items.text: Assess oxygen saturation and response to supplemental oxygen.
choices: Hypoxemic, corrected with low-flow supplemental oxygen
Hypoxemic despite high-flow oxygen or a non-rebreather mask

items.text: Auscultate for stridor.
Auscultate the lungs for focal versus diffuse findings.
choices: Focal crackles or dullness in one region

items.text: Assess jugular venous pressure.
choices: Elevated jugular venous pressure with prominent v waves

items.text: Auscultate heart sounds for a gallop.
choices: Normal S1 and S2, no extra sounds

items.text: Auscultate the heart for a new murmur.
Assess for pulsus paradoxus and Beck triad findings suggesting tamponade.
choices: No pulsus paradoxus, heart sounds normal
Hypotension with elevated jugular venous pressure and muffled heart sounds

items.text: Assess tracheal position and breath sounds for asymmetry.
choices: Unilateral decreased or absent breath sounds
Tracheal deviation with hyperresonance to percussion

items.text: Inspect the face and skin for urticaria or angioedema.
Assess mental status.
Assess lips and nail beds for central cyanosis.
Inspect and palpate the calves for asymmetric swelling or a palpable cord.
choices: Unilateral swelling with tenderness or a palpable cord

items.text: Inspect the conjunctiva and nail beds for pallor.
